events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need diagnose fault: splice outside box, in-service, terminations & splices. NEC 110.14 covers electrical connections - splices must be in approved enclosures/boxes, accessible, proper termination, torque, etc. Splice outside box is non-compliant, likely code violation and safety hazard: exposed conductors, risk of shock, arc fault, fire. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Maybe degraded? Since splice outside box could be degraded insulation. But categories that may apply: degraded, failed. The schema component_conditions includes installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. We should include non-compliant, installed-defective. Maybe failed? The splice itself not necessarily failed electrically but installation defective. Could include degraded if insulation damaged. But evidence only splice outside box. Use non-compliant, installed-defective. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? Exposed energized splice in service: critical due to shock/arc/fire. But maybe high. Let's assess. In-service operating asset, splice outside box means accessible? If exposed and energized, severe hazard. Critical seems appropriate. Work stage in-service. percent_complete 100 (installed operating). Findings: splice made outside approved enclosure; exposed/unprotected conductor; violation of NEC 110.14; shock and fire hazard. Actions: de-energize circuit, install approved junction box/enclosure, relocate splice into enclosure with proper connectors, inspect terminations torque, test. Rationale: NEC requires splices/terminations in listed enclosures suitable for environment; splice outside box lacks protection and accessibility compliance. References: NEC 110.14, maybe 300.15? But reference material only NEC 110.14. We can include NEC 110.14. Also maybe NEC 314? But task says reference material on hand NEC 110.14. Use that. Need valid JSON with all fields. Ensure no prose outside.

    "Exposed/unprotected termination subject to physical damage and accidental contact",
    "Non-compliant installation creating shock, arc-flash, and fire hazard",
    "Violation of NEC 110.14 requirements for proper termination and enclosure protection"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize and LOTO the affected circuit before handling",
    "Install an approved junction/box or enclosure suitable for the location and conductor size",
    "Relocate the splice into the enclosure using proper connectors and strain relief",
    "Verify conductor terminations are clean, properly matched, and torqued to OEM/UL specifications",
    "Re-insulate, close, and label the enclosure; restore power and test"
  ],
  "rationale": "An in-service splice made outside a box has no physical or environmental protection and violates NEC 110.14, which requires terminations and splices to be made in approved enclosures under proper conditions. This presents an immediate shock, arc-flash, and fire risk, so the installation is failed and must be corrected.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14"
  ]
}
